Kizer as the first witness. Filig.
nos case was chosen for trial first
and In it's connection Kizer testi
fled to acknowledgements of guilt

He told how he had asked each
of the men why they did not en-
ploy other means of defeating the
ordinance If they wished 1t invali
dated In order that they could
Speak on the streets, and said that
Filigno told him that he chose to

kizer testified that Wilson de-
clared he had forgotten the names
of all the executive committee. had
forgotten whether he had ever told
or encouraged anyone to speak on
the streets In violation of the or.
dipance and that he claimed to
have forgotten about everything
else than that he was editor of the
Industrial Worker, which he free
ly admitted. He testified that in
each instance, Cousins, Wilson and
Filigno acknowledged their con.
seriousness of the existence of the
ordinance against street speaking
and that what they had done to
get men to come to Spokane and
to promote the fight was to break
the ordinance.


Following is the substance OF

The sixth offered as evidence was
sent by Filigno to San Francisco
revolutionists, and follows, Speech
flight on. Many arrested. Send
men ready"


Filignos case, with the testimony
Offered by Jones, or rather the tele
